file_permissions_unauthorized_world_writ.scope: This SSG file-permission rule is not part of the DISA V2R8 RHEL9 STIG profile, but it is directly applicable to image-rootfs integrity and is retained as an extra signal.

file_permissions_unauthorized_suid.scope: This SSG file-permission rule is not part of the DISA V2R8 RHEL9 STIG profile, but unexpected SUID files are directly applicable to a distroless image.

file_permissions_unauthorized_sgid.scope: This SSG file-permission rule is not part of the DISA V2R8 RHEL9 STIG profile, but unexpected SGID files are directly applicable to a distroless image.

omission_groups.justification: Authselect and graphical login-banner requirements configure an interactive host login stack. ubi9-base-micro has no shell login, PAM stack, GDM, or init system.

These controls govern vendor support checks, mutable host patch workflow, local banners, bootloader settings, kernel/sysctl state, systemd services, and host FIPS policy. The image rootfs is instead controlled by digest-pinned UBI inputs, dual CVE gates, FIPS module gates, and the consuming host/runtime.

Partition and mount-option controls are properties of the consuming host/container runtime mount namespace, not static image rootfs bytes. The image does not define host partitions, NFS/removable media, /boot, or /dev/shm mounts.

These controls target local interactive user initialization files and home directories. The base-provided nonroot account uses /sbin/nologin and has /home/nonroot mode 0700; because the account cannot log in, interactive-home controls do not apply.

These controls target cron configuration directories and crontab files. ubi9-base-micro ships no cron daemon or cron configuration tree; cron scheduling is a consuming host or platform responsibility.

These controls target audit tool binaries and /etc/audit configuration paths. The distroless runtime image ships no auditd stack, audit tools, or audit configuration authority; audit policy belongs to the consuming host.

These controls target host log directories and /var/log/messages ownership or mode. The image does not ship a persistent system log tree or syslog message file; logging paths are runtime host or platform state.
SELinux device-file labeling is a property of the consuming host kernel, policy, and device namespace, not a static image-rootfs byte claim.

These controls rely on host SELinux state, system crypto-policy tooling, package manager state, or mutable system configuration. The image evidence for this step is static rootfs scanning; FIPS, RPM provenance, and CVE policy are enforced by separate build-failing gates.
Audit daemon policy, log retention, audit rules, and log forwarding are runtime host responsibilities. The image has no auditd, journald service, persistent audit volume, or systemd unit graph.

Firewall, network service, and kernel network settings are enforced by the consuming runtime and cluster/network policy. The distroless image does not run host network daemons or configure host firewall state.

Password aging, PAM, faillock, sudo/su, smartcard, SSH daemon, and remote-login controls require an interactive host authentication stack. ubi9-base-micro runs as fixed USER 65532 and ships no shell, sshd, PAM login path, sudo, or su.

These controls govern host package verification, AIDE/session/cron-like services, or mutable host security agents. The image evidence is covered by digest-pinned base inputs, rpmdb-preserving SBOM/CVE gates, rootfs secret checks, and the selected rootfs permission rules.

The remaining DISA residual controls in this release are host or operational controls outside static image-rootfs authority. They are not marked passing by the image ARF; they are explicitly scoped to the consuming platform.
